title: rooms/room_actions/match_scores carry player-supplied names with no data-classification annotation, unlike rate_limits
evidence: 005_rate_limits.sql line 27 explicitly tags its table: `-- classification: internal (no PII; derived IP + counters only)`. No such annotation exists on `rooms` (001_init.sql, whose `players` JSONB column stores a free-text `name` field per the shape comment at line 18: `players: Array<{ id, name, color, ready }>`), `room_actions`, or `match_scores` (whose `scoreboard` JSONB embeds `playerName`, per its shape comment at line 18). These are the only tables in the schema holding user-supplied free text, yet they are the ones without a classification comment.
impact: Without a stated classification, a future migration or Edge Function change could treat these columns as non-sensitive by default (e.g. logging them verbosely, exposing them in a new public read path) with no annotation to prompt a second look. Low impact today since `name`/`playerName` are self-chosen display handles for a casual game with no auth, but the inconsistency undermines the classification convention 005 established.
recommendation: Add a one-line classification comment to 001_init.sql (rooms, room_actions) and 003_match_scores.sql mirroring 005's style, e.g. `-- classification: internal (player-chosen display name, no verified PII)`.
acceptance_criteria: rooms, room_actions, and match_scores each carry a classification comment consistent with 005_rate_limits.sql's convention
